textPlain: > "This is another victory in the fight against censorship," Jordan wrote in a post on X.Thank God Republicans are fighting against censorship. Without their efforts people might have gotten banned for posting actual Charlie Kirk quotes following his recent, tragic death.
2 years is a pretty long ban for a not even illegal conduct.Although if they got banned during the start of covid during the Trump administration then we're talking about 5 years.
What exactly constituted a violation of a COVID policy?
I think hardware and ip level bans.. should be banned.I know that some services do this in addition to account ban.
Free speech for everyone except the haters of the current administration and their near & dear ones! </s>
That will teach people to stop spreading propaganda! Lol.
Why would you want con men on your platform at all? Why not a permanent ban?

it was not actually policies - they just booted channels they were told to boot. that's actually the spicy part of the story.
Because its left/right politics thing though... you'll see up/down votes for all sort of other things.

According to Google's censorship algorithm, Michael Osterholm's podcast (famous epidemiologist and, at the time, a member of President Biden's own gold-star covid-19 advisory panel).https://x.com/cidrap/status/1420482621696618496 ("Our Osterholm Update podcast episode (Jul 22) was removed for “medical misinformation.”" (2021))Most ironic thing I've ever seen. I still recall it perfectly, though it's been four years. Never, ever trust algorithms or the people who control them: they are just dumb machines that suppress all discussion of an unwanted topic, without nuance or reason.

Every opinion different from the opinion of "authorities". They documented it here:https://blog.youtube/news-and-events/managing-harmful-vaccin...From the two links in the post, Google fleshes it out in great detail, with many examples of forbidden thought.
Two articles that I found offered a well-rounded analysis:- https://www.engadget.com/big-tech/youtube-may-reinstate-chan...- https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2025/09/youtube-will-restore...
Far too many people are free speech hypocrites.
